title: Easy-read / low-literacy checklist variants
context: Some community repair event attendees and volunteer fixers may have limited literacy or cognitive disabilities. Easy-read variants of core triage checklists — using plain language, short sentences, and high-contrast print-legible formatting — extend the kit's reach. These variants must meet the same citation, license, no-self-approval, and accessibility requirements as standard units, and must additionally pass an accessibility review by a reviewer with expertise in easy-read / plain-language standards (e.g. Mencap Easy Read guidelines or equivalent). Content license: CC-BY-4.0.
objective: Produce easy-read / low-literacy variants of at least 2 core triage checklists, using plain language, short sentences, and accessible formatting, reviewed by an approver with accessibility and plain-language expertise distinct from the author.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 easy-read variants of existing core triage checklists are produced, using plain language (short sentences, common words, active voice), minimal jargon, and supportive formatting

Each variant is reviewed by an approver with accessibility and plain-language expertise (e.g. knowledge of Mencap Easy Read guidelines or equivalent), distinct from the author; a PR-tied review-log entry is written
Variants meet print-legibility requirements: WCAG AA contrast, minimum font size, no color-only meaning, and are suitable for printing in low-connectivity venues
Each variant conforms to the data-002 schema, passes the CI content-governance gate, and is published under CC-BY-4.0
No third-party logos, trademarks, or the 'Repair Café' name appear in any variant
output: At least 2 committed, schema-valid easy-read triage checklist variants (CC-BY-4.0), reviewed by an accessibility/plain-language expert distinct from the author, with PR-tied review-log entries.
